report_name: Unit 42 Details Techniques for Abusing AWS and Google Cloud Logging Services for Defense Evasion

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs
attack_patterns.description: An attacker disables AWS CloudTrail logging via the `StopLogging` API call to cease the recording of API events and create a visibility gap.

attack_patterns.name: File Deletion
attack_patterns.description: An attacker with sufficient permissions deletes the Amazon S3 bucket used for log storage, causing log delivery to fail.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: An attacker deletes the log routing configuration, such as an AWS CloudTrail `Trail` object or a Google Cloud `Sink`, to break the connection between event generation and log storage.

attack_patterns.name: Data Manipulation
attack_patterns.description: An attacker renders logs unreadable by changing the log encryption key to one they control and then revoking the victim's access to that key.

indicators.name: AWS CloudTrail Logging Disabled or Tampered
indicators.pattern:
title: AWS CloudTrail Logging Disabled or Tampered
id: 2b8a1c3d-f4e5-4b9a-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects attempts to disable or tamper with AWS CloudTrail logging by stopping a trail, deleting a trail, or modifying its configuration. This is a strong indicator of defense evasion.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cloud-logging-defense-evasion/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1562.008
  - attack.t1562.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  product: aws
  service: cloudtrail
detection:
  selection:
    eventName:
      - StopLogging
      - DeleteTrail
      - UpdateTrail
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects API calls used to stop or delete AWS CloudTrail logging configurations, indicating a defense evasion attempt.

indicators.name: GCP Cloud Logging Sink Tampered
indicators.pattern:
title: GCP Cloud Logging Sink Tampered
id: 9e8f7d6c-5b4a-4c3d-2b1a-0f9e8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects attempts to tamper with Google Cloud Logging by deleting or updating a log sink. This is a strong indicator of defense evasion.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cloud-logging-defense-evasion/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1562.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  product: gcp
  service: audit
detection:
  selection:
    protoPayload.methodName:
      - google.logging.v2.ConfigServiceV2.DeleteSink
      - google.logging.v2.ConfigServiceV2.UpdateSink
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects API calls used to delete or modify Google Cloud log sinks, indicating a defense evasion attempt.

indicators.name: AWS S3 Log Storage Bucket Deleted
indicators.pattern:
title: AWS S3 Log Storage Bucket Deleted
id: 7c6b5a49-3d2b-4a19-8f0e-9d8c7b6a5948
status: experimental
description: Detects the deletion of an S3 bucket. If this bucket is known to store security logs (e.g., CloudTrail), this is a high-fidelity indicator of defense evasion.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cloud-logging-defense-evasion/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
falsepositives:
  - Deletion of non-log-related S3 buckets.
notes: This rule should be scoped to specific, known log buckets in a production environment to reduce noise.
tags:
  - attack.t1070.004
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  product: aws
  service: cloudtrail
detection:
  selection:
    eventName: DeleteBucket
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the deletion of an S3 bucket, which could be an attempt to remove stored logs.

indicators.name: AWS KMS Key for Log Encryption Scheduled for Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: AWS KMS Key for Log Encryption Scheduled for Deletion
id: 5a493d2b-1a98-4f0e-7d6c-5b4a39281706
status: experimental
description: Detects attempts to schedule the deletion of a KMS key. If the key is used for encrypting logs, this can render them unreadable, achieving defense evasion.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cloud-logging-defense-evasion/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1565
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  product: aws
  service: cloudtrail
detection:
  selection:
    eventSource: kms.amazonaws.com
    eventName: ScheduleKeyDeletion
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the scheduling of deletion for a KMS key, which could be used to make encrypted logs unreadable.

indicators.name: Cloud Logging Tampering via CLI
indicators.pattern:
title: Cloud Logging Tampering via CLI
id: 1d0c9b8a-7e6d-4c5b-a493-8d7c6b5a4938
status: experimental
description: Detects command-line execution of tools used to tamper with cloud logging configurations in AWS and GCP.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cloud-logging-defense-evasion/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1562.008
  - attack.t1562.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection_aws:
    Image|endswith:
      - '/aws'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'cloudtrail stop-logging'
      - 'cloudtrail delete-trail'
  selection_gcp:
    Image|endswith:
      - '/gcloud'
    CommandLine|contains: 'logging sinks delete'
  condition: 1 of them
indicators.description: Detects command-line interface (CLI) commands used to disable or delete cloud logging resources in AWS or GCP.

relationships.source_name: AWS CloudTrail Logging Disabled or Tampered
AWS CloudTrail Logging Disabled or Tampered
AWS S3 Log Storage Bucket Deleted
AWS KMS Key for Log Encryption Scheduled for Deletion
